roadmap.vision: Build the Gyro ecosystem from theory to runtime, application, project operations, and research platform.

phases.name: Theory
phases.status: Mathematical Formalization Study Completed / Paper Preparation Ready
future_work: README and README_jp update after terminology review
phases.next: Reconstruct Minimal Formal Model v1 into paper architecture, then review terminology and cross-document consistency before README, papers, and release candidate updates

phases.name: Runtime
phases.next: Continue the current v4.0 PoC without introducing the deferred runtime-positioning study

phases.name: Application
phases.status: Publication Milestones Completed / GyroAuth Formalization Study Active
completed: Criterion Integrity and Criterion Trajectory candidate structure
future_work: Criterion update audit and rollback model
Primary and applied paper next-version planning
phases.next: Keep the new concepts as research candidates until GyroAuth creates and reviews formal documents; do not add candidate fields to canonical schemas yet

phases.name: Ecosystem
phases.next: Reflect project updates and maintain weekly continuity

phases.name: Automation
phases.next: Evaluate document, model-version, artifact, and paper-reflection tooling without fixing exploratory theory into canonical schemas

future_research_themes.name: Mathematical Foundations
future_research_themes.objective: Provide a minimal integrated formal schema for Gyro Logic while preserving the invariant Core and avoiding reduction to one existing mathematical field.
future_research_themes.current_model: Gyro Logic Minimal Formal Model v1
completed_milestones: Grade S Structure, Slice, and Stability studies
Grade A Incorporated Readability, Continuity Readability, Trajectory, and Difference studies
future_research_themes.boundary: Minimal Formal Model v1 is an integrated provisional model, not a final axiomatization. It does not identify Gyro Logic with one mathematical discipline and does not change Structure → Slice → Stability or the canonical Core definitions.

future_research_themes.name: Gyro Runtime Architecture
future_research_themes.status: Deferred while GyroOS v4.0 PoC is active
future_research_themes.objective: Clarify GyroOS runtime positioning after the current PoC phase.
future_research_themes.boundary: Do not introduce this theme into the current GyroOS v4.0 PoC unless the GyroOS project explicitly reopens it.

future_research_themes.name: Trajectory-Based Authentication and Criterion Integrity
future_research_themes.objective: Formalize what GyroAuth selects and how it evaluates both the authentication relation and the integrity of the changing criterion used for that selection.
future_research_themes.current_definition: Authentication = Stability-based Selection over State Convergence
future_research_themes.boundary: All new concepts, fields, responses, and formulas remain candidates until GyroAuth formally documents and approves them. Gyro Logic Core and GyroOS Runtime remain unchanged.

future_research_themes.name: Theory Identity
future_research_themes.status: Advanced through Minimal Formal Model v1
future_research_themes.objective: Clarify the primary object and explanatory scope of Gyro Logic without reducing the theory to one object type or mathematical field.
future_research_themes.current_position: Gyro Logic integrates local Structure realization, Slice process, Stability scene, incorporated readability, continuity readability, Trajectory tracing, and structured Difference while retaining the invariant Core.
future_research_themes.boundary: This position is an integrated interpretation and provisional model, not a new Core definition.
